questions.objective_ref: Define what an AI agent is and explain how it differs from a standalone LLM responding to a single prompt.
Identify the five core components of an agent loop — goal, planning, tool use, memory, and feedback — and describe the role of each.
Explain why adding planning and tool use to an LLM transforms it from a 'question-answerer' into an autonomous actor.
Describe at least two real-world scenarios where an agent architecture is more useful than a single-prompt LLM call.
Recognize the current limitations of AI agents — where they tend to fail and why human oversight remains important.
